signal.text: July 2026: 223 articles collected. Dominant topic: INDUSTRY (46 articles). Most active sources: TechCrunch, Theregister.com, Plos.org. 15 peer-reviewed papers included.

articles.title: Understanding the structure of swine leukocyte antigen class I molecules
articles.summary: This study examines the structural characteristics of swine leukocyte antigen (SLA) class I molecules, which are important for understanding immune recognition in swine. The research was conducted by investigators from Dalian University and affiliated institutions in China, with findings relevant to veterinary immunology and vaccine development.

articles.title: Oral microbiome modulation mitigates hyperglycemia exacerbation in gestational diabetes mellitus
articles.summary: Here, the authors associate changes in the oral microbiome during gestational diabetes with gum inflammation and blood sugar control, and show in animal models and a human trial that restoring healthier oral bacteria or topical docosahexaenoic acid, reduce th…

articles.title: How proteins fold
articles.summary: Despite advances in protein structure prediction, how proteins fold remains unclear. Understanding protein (mis)folding will require single-molecule techniques and advanced computation tools.

articles.title: Targeted enzyme discovery using metal-coordination mining
articles.summary: A methodology for mining protein structure databases on the basis of the distinct intrinsic structures of metal-binding active sites in enzymes enables the discovery of new families of radical halogenases.

articles.title: Towards the construction of a virtual yeast
articles.summary: A virtual yeast, an artificial intelligence-driven agent for modelling eukaryotic cellular behaviours, is described.

articles.title: U2af1 mutations rescue deleterious exon skipping induced by kras mutations
articles.summary: KRASG12S mutations generate potential docking sites for splicing factor U2AF1, which can lead to exon skipping and nonfunctional KRAS transcripts. This is mitigated by mutations in U2AF1, which rescue exon skipping, thereby restoring KRAS activity in lung ade…

articles.title: Biological aging might help to explain the rising risk of early-onset cancer
articles.summary: Cancers have risen rapidly in younger adults in recent generations. Analyzing large population-based cohorts, we found that recent generations exhibited signs of faster biological aging than older ones, and that systemic and organ-specific aging markers were …

articles.title: Trop2 targeting reveals therapy-driven cell state dynamics in colorectal cancer
articles.summary: TROP2 marks poor-prognosis colorectal cancer cells with fetal-like, therapy-resistant states, and therapeutic targeting of TROP2 with antibody–drug conjugates in combination with chemotherapy enhances treatment efficacy by modulating tumour cell plasticity an…

articles.title: Improved heritability partitioning and enrichment analyses using summary statistics with graphreml
articles.summary: graphREML is a likelihood-based heritability partitioning method that uses genome-wide association studies summary statistics and linkage disequilibrium graphical models. It yields enrichment estimates concordant with S-LDSC but shows greater power and produc…

articles.title: Rnabpflow: base pair-augmented se(3) flow matching for conditional rna 3d structure generation
articles.summary: RNAbpFlow generates all-atom RNA conformational ensembles for single-chain RNA monomers without the explicit or implicit use of evolutionary information or homologous structural templates.

articles.title: Retraction note: nsd2 targeting reverses plasticity and drug resistance in prostate cancer
articles.summary: Nature - Retraction Note: NSD2 targeting reverses plasticity and drug resistance in prostate cancer

articles.title: Imaging the hallmarks of cancer
articles.summary: In this Review, Grimm et al. describe the range of non-invasive diagnostic imaging approaches to monitor the various hallmarks of cancer and how their implementation is shaping clinical practice.

articles.title: EGFR-dependent delivery of phosphorylated IGFBP3 by cancer stem cells drives chemotherapy resistance in colorectal cancer
articles.summary: Cancer stem cells in colorectal cancer secrete phosphorylated IGFBP3 protein through an EGFR-dependent mechanism, which promotes chemotherapy resistance in tumor cells. This finding identifies a novel pathway by which cancer stem cells communicate with bulk tumor cells to reduce drug sensitivity. The study suggests that targeting this EGFR-IGFBP3 signaling axis may enhance chemotherapy efficacy in colorectal cancer.

articles.title: Daily briefing: editing the epigenome with crispr to treat disease
articles.summary: CRISPR’s next act, the new ‘world’s fastest computer’ and how to triumph in the metaphorical penalty shootout of life.

articles.title: Real-world comparative outcomes of darolutamide- versus abiraterone-based triplet therapy for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer: a retrospective cohort study
articles.summary: This retrospective cohort study compared real-world clinical outcomes between darolutamide- and abiraterone-based triplet therapy regimens in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer. The analysis evaluated the effectiveness and safety of these two androgen receptor inhibitor approaches when combined with standard hormone therapy in a clinical practice setting.

articles.title: Ciliation and mucus secretion in an in vitro model of nasal mucosa based on CI-pNaEC cell line for preclinical drug testing
articles.summary: Researchers developed an in vitro nasal mucosa model using the CI-pNaEC cell line to evaluate ciliation and mucus secretion for preclinical drug testing. This model system enables assessment of key functional characteristics of nasal tissue relevant to pharmaceutical development and safety evaluation.

articles.title: Organoid-AI platforms need integrated governance in drug discovery
articles.summary: Organoid-AI platforms are functioning as integrated decision systems in drug discovery, influencing compound prioritization, toxicity assessment, and program progression. Current governance approaches validate the biological and computational components separately, despite their interdependent roles in these systems. Integrated governance frameworks are needed to address the combined decision-making functions of organoid-AI platforms in drug development.

articles.summary: AI trade signals weaken as pricing power declines, raising concerns over future profitability and market sustainability.

articles.title: Icas members call for bigger government role in AI regulation
articles.summary: An ICAS survey found that 60% of CAs want greater government intervention in AI regulation.

articles.title: AI law may be considered as time is getting right, export curbs eased on mythos: meity secretary s krishnan
articles.summary: The Centre may begin work on a dedicated legal framework for artificial intelligence (AI) as the technology continues to evolve, Secretary, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), S Krishnan, said on Friday, indicating that the government …

articles.title: Review on drug repositioning of some FDA-approved drugs for treatment of cancer/microbial diseases
articles.summary: This review examines the repositioning of FDA-approved drugs for treatment of cancer and microbial diseases. Drug repositioning leverages pre-existing safety and pharmacokinetic data from approved medications, substantially reducing development timelines and costs compared to de novo drug development.

articles.title: People behind the progress: preparing us for what comes next
articles.summary: The TechUK President’s Awards celebrate individuals across the tech sector who are helping to transform the world we live in for the better

articles.title: Pre-deployment simulations are vital to developing generative AI that can best provide mental health advice
articles.summary: Pre-deployment simulation is a new technique from OpenAI. It can be used to better shape AI-led mental health guidance. An AI Insider analysis and scoop.

articles.title: Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) immunotherapy reprograms CNS immunity and alters Alzheimer's biomarkers: results from two open-label clinical trials
articles.summary: Two open-label clinical trials demonstrate that Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) immunotherapy modulates central nervous system immunity and produces measurable changes in Alzheimer's disease biomarkers. These findings suggest BCG may alter the immune environment in the brain through a reprogramming mechanism relevant to neurodegenerative disease pathology.

articles.title: Dissecting autonomous enzymatic variability in single cells
articles.summary: Researchers used single-cell analysis to characterize the inherent variability in enzyme activity across individual cells, providing insights into how enzymatic function differs at the cellular level independent of external factors.

articles.title: Doctors miss about 12 million diagnoses per year — here is what AI can actually fix
articles.summary: 12 million missed diagnoses per year, 40,000–80,000 preventable deaths. AI is helping most in imaging. Here's where the evidence supports AI diagnostic assistance — and where it doesn't.

articles.title: America at 250: navigating u.s. AI policy and the digital overhaul of the textile industry
articles.summary: Discover how AI is transforming the textile industry with automation, sustainability, and evolving U.S. regulatory policies.

articles.title: Ferrocene-based Drug Molecules in Medicinal Chemistry: Comprehensive Insights into their Chemistry, Pharmacology, and Emerging Therapeutic Applications
articles.summary: Ferrocene is an organometallic compound discovered in 1951 that continues to be of interest in medicinal chemistry due to its exceptional stability and has emerging applications as a basis for drug molecules. This comprehensive review examines the chemistry, pharmacology, and therapeutic potential of ferrocene-based drug molecules in medicinal chemistry.

articles.title: Kpmg ran an internal exam to certify staff on the ethical use of artificial intelligence — 28 employees were caught using artificial intelligence to cheat on it, including a partner who was fined $10,000
articles.summary: KPMG Australia confirmed in February 2026 that 28 of its staff had used artificial intelligence tools to cheat on internal training exams since July 2025, after the firm introduced monitoring systems specifically designed to detect unauthorised AI use during …

articles.title: Gabapentin Extended Release Tablets in Healthy Subjects Under Fed Conditions: A Randomized, Open-Label, Two-Treatment, Two-Period, Two-Sequence, Single Dose, Crossover, Comparative Bioavailability Study
articles.summary: This randomized, open-label, crossover bioavailability study compared gabapentin extended-release tablets in healthy subjects under fed conditions using a two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence design with single-dose administration. The study was conducted by Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited and collaborating institutions to establish comparative bioavailability data for the formulation. Published in Drugs R&D, the research provides pharmacokinetic characterization of gabapentin extended-release tablets relevant to regulatory assessment and formulation development.

articles.title: Cytotoxicity and synergistic anticancer activity of Kigelia africana (Lam.) Benth. and Spathodea campanulata P. Beauv. polyherbal extracts
articles.summary: This study evaluated the cytotoxic and anticancer properties of polyherbal extracts combining Kigelia africana and Spathodea campanulata, investigating their individual and synergistic effects against cancer cell lines. The research demonstrates that these plant extracts exhibit cytotoxic activity and produce enhanced anticancer effects when used in combination. Findings suggest potential therapeutic applications of these polyherbal formulations in cancer treatment.

articles.title: FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Young Children with Sickle Cell Disease
articles.summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a supplemental approval for Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) for patients aged 2 years and older with either sickle cell disease (SCD) with recurrent vaso-occlusive crises (VOCs) or transfusion-dependent β thalassemia (TDT).

articles.summary: The first wave of  artificial intelligence (AI) regulation focused on what goes into models. California’s Training Data Transparency Act, which went into effect Jan. 1, requires developers to disclose their training data, including sources, collection periods…

articles.summary: The CREATE AI Act doesn't pick winners, doesn't burden industry and already has a proven track record — Capitol Hill's most actionable AI legislation.

articles.summary: Anthropic, OpenAI, the Vatican and Congress all agree AI needs guardrails—but they disagree on what should be protected first, from catastrophic risk to human dignity and U.S. competitiveness.

articles.summary: As AI cyber threats grow, organizations need trained teams, not just stronger tools.

articles.title: AI will drive africa’s sustainable development, conference declares
articles.summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has been identified as a critical catalyst for accelerating innovation, strengthening governance, improving institutional resilience and advancing sustainable development across Africa and the Global South, as experts at an intern…

articles.title: Ophthalmic self-medication among adult ophthalmic patients attending a tertiary eye care centre in Northwest Ethiopia: a mixed-methods study assessing prevalence, associated factors and lived experience
articles.summary: This mixed-methods study evaluated the prevalence of self-medication practices for eye conditions among adult patients at a tertiary eye care centre in Northwest Ethiopia, identifying associated risk factors and documenting patients' firsthand experiences with self-treatment. The research combined quantitative prevalence data with qualitative insights to understand the scope and drivers of ophthalmic self-medication in this population.

articles.title: Safety evaluation of CD3×CD20 bispecific antibodies: a pharmacovigilance study using FDA adverse event reporting system
articles.summary: This pharmacovigilance study analyzed adverse event reports from the FDA system to evaluate the safety profile of CD3×CD20 bispecific antibodies, a class of immunotherapeutic agents designed to engage both T cells and B cells in cancer treatment. The research systematically identified and characterized safety signals associated with these bispecific antibodies using real-world post-market surveillance data. The findings provide regulatory and clinical insights into the adverse event patterns and safety considerations for this emerging therapeutic class.

articles.title: Collaborative development of a digital intervention to support opioid tapering after surgery in primary care: an experience-based co-design study with patients and clinicians
articles.summary: Researchers used experience-based co-design methodology involving patients and clinicians to develop a digital intervention aimed at supporting opioid tapering in primary care following surgical procedures. The collaborative approach integrated patient and healthcare provider perspectives to create a tool designed to address post-operative opioid management in the primary care setting.

articles.title: FDA Approves New Treatment That Uses Donor Immune Cells to Prevent Serious Complications in Blood Cancer Patients
articles.summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved TREGZI, the first regulatory T (Treg) cell-based immunotherapy for improving chronic graft-versus-host disease (GVHD)-free survival in adult patients with blood cancers undergoing allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT).

articles.summary: This study examines the current infrastructure and development needs of drug clinical trial institutions across Sichuan Province in China. The research, authored by investigators from West China Second University Hospital and affiliated research laboratories, provides an assessment of institutional capacity and identifies requirements for strengthening clinical trial operations in the region.
